overall.description: A static codex entry screen from Assassin's Creed Odyssey's Discovery Tour mode, displaying educational text about ancient Greek fishing methods alongside a terracotta plate artifact with fish relief carvings. The scene remains fixed on the museum-style information panel as text gradually reveals details about Mediterranean navigation and fishing techniques.

scene_state.functional_zone: museum codex information display with ancient artifact

agents.ego_description: player character stationary, reading the educational codex entry about ancient Greek fishing methods and examining the terracotta plate artifact

entered.detail: round terracotta plate with raised relief carvings depicting fish and sea creatures, typical of ancient Greek pottery
textured stone or plaster wall serving as the backdrop for the museum display
scrolling text panel titled 'Fishing Methods' containing educational content about ancient Mediterranean navigation and fishing techniques
interactive UI button labeled 'MORE INFO' at the bottom left of the screen
interactive UI button labeled 'CLOSE' positioned next to the more info button

segments.scene_description: The segment shows a static museum codex screen from a video game, focusing on a terracotta fishing plate with detailed relief carvings of marine life. Educational text about ancient Greek fishing methods scrolls gradually on the left side of the screen against a stone wall background, while the player observes the artifact without any camera movement or interaction.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot presents a static museum codex screen that maintains a focused, educational atmosphere throughout its duration. It begins with a detailed view of a terracotta fishing plate featuring intricate relief carvings of marine life set against a textured stone wall. As the camera remains fixed, educational text regarding ancient Greek fishing methods gradually scrolls upward on the left side of the frame. The scene then settles into a steady observation of the artifact, allowing the information to reveal itself fully without any shift in perspective or interaction.
